SKILLS
TACTICB" character with the Tactics Skill is schooled in the art of war: Unlike the Strategy Skill, which deals with war and logistics large scale this skili deals with individual troopers and machines as well as small units up to company Or So in Size Characters with the Tactics Skill May add their skill bonus their Initiative total provided the combat situ: ation appropriate their Tactics knowledge _ For example: character with only Tactics/ 'Mech would gain no bonus t0 Initiative in combat outside his 'Mech;

Technician Subskills These subskills are not strictly vehicular; tne Technician / Ballistics Skill covers the repair of slug-throwing personal weapons well BattleMech autocannon. Aeronautics:   Fixed-wing   flight Sys tems found on fighters and aerodyne DropShips and spacecraft_ Ballistics: Includes machine gun, Gauss rifle autocannon and tube artillery weapon systems Communications Comms): Sensors and communications gear, including ECM and probe systems. Electronics: Includes tar geting systems computers and control systems for most modern components_ Fusion: Fusion engines including extra light (XL), for ground-based vehicles and aero space fighters_ ICE: Internal   combustion engines for ground vehicles_ Interplanetary: Large reaction dri- ves found DropShips and WarShips including the fusion engines that power them; Jet: Jet-drive systems including fighter engines and BattleMech jump jets Jump Drive: KF jump drive systems_ including Iithium-fusion batteries_ Lasers: Laser weapon systems including pulse laser and PPC weapon systems_ Mechanics: Includes internal struc turc; armor and ground vehicle drive systems Missile: Includes missile and missile artillery weapon systems Myomer: BattleMech and battle armor  musculature_ including actua tors
Tactics Subskills The Tactics Subskills are based on the various theaters ofbattle in the  thirty-first century; summa rized below: Alr: Combat in the atmosphere of a planet; whether in aerospace conventional fighters_ DropShips and VTOLs when used conjunction with air forces 'Mech: Any combat   involving BattleMechs in any environment; Infantry: Combat involving con- ventional and/or battle-armored Intantry: Space: Combat in space_ includ" aerospace fighters, DropShips_ JumpShips and WarShips Ground: Vehicular combat land,  including tank, hovercraft and Wheeled  vehicles well VTOLs wen used conjunction with ground forces_ Naval:  Vehicular combat on the surface and underwater; including conventional naval vessels hydrofoils and submarines_
